ingredients.description: Number of relativistic dark species at BBN/recombination
Decoupling temperature of dark sector from SM bath
Entropy-transfer history between dark and visible sector
Whether dark photons/gluons/neutrinos exist and are relativistic at BBN
ingredients.note: AUTHOR_HINTED — existence implied, thermal properties absent

ingredients.description: Coupling/thermalization assumptions between dark and SM sectors
Explicit calculation or bound for ΔN_eff
Standard ΔN_eff formula (external physics — for reference only)
ingredients.note: EXTERNAL_STANDARD_PHYSICS — cannot compute without IDM inputs I1-I6

standard_formula.reason: T_dark, g_D, T_dark/T_ν all NOT_FOUND in preprint

questions_for_author: Q_TH1: Were the five dark isomers ever thermally populated in the early universe?
Q_TH2: What is T_dark/T_visible for each dark sector?
Q_TH3: At what temperature did the dark sector decouple from the SM bath?
Q_TH4: Which dark-sector particles are relativistic at BBN (T~MeV) and at recombination (T~eV)?
Q_TH5: What ΔN_eff does IDM predict, and how does it compare to Planck 2018?
